agent_output:
{
  "domain_analysis": {
    "business_context": "New 'Express Delivery' product offering faster delivery with a time-bound SLA and premium fee, layered on top of an existing standard delivery service. The product involves eligibility constraints (distance, driver rating), operational commitments (pickup windows, driver reassignment), and a refund policy for missed delivery windows.",
    "bounded_context": "Express Delivery — a sub-domain of Order Fulfillment that introduces time-sensitive delivery commitments, express-specific eligibility rules, driver qualification requirements, and a dedicated refund policy distinct from standard delivery.",
    "ubiquitous_language": [
      { "term": "Express Delivery", "definition": "A premium delivery option with a target delivery window of 2 hours from order placement, available to eligible customers for an additional fee." },
      { "term": "Express Fee", "definition": "A fixed surcharge of $12.99 charged to the customer for selecting express delivery, separate from the base delivery fee." },
      { "term": "Base Delivery Fee", "definition": "The standard delivery charge that applies to all orders regardless of delivery tier. Not subject to express refund policy." },
      { "term": "Delivery Window", "definition": "The 2-hour target timeframe within which an express delivery should be completed, starting from order placement." },
      { "term": "Pickup SLA", "definition": "The 15-minute maximum time from order placement to driver pickup at the warehouse. Exceeding this jeopardizes the delivery window." },
      { "term": "Express-Eligible Customer", "definition": "A customer whose delivery address is within 10km of a warehouse, making them eligible to select the express delivery option." },
      { "term": "Express-Qualified Driver", "definition": "A driver with a rating of 4.0 stars or above who is eligible to fulfill express deliveries." },
      { "term": "Missed Window", "definition": "A condition where the express delivery is not completed within the 2-hour delivery window, potentially triggering a goodwill refund of the express fee." },
      { "term": "Goodwill Refund", "definition": "A non-contractual refund of the express fee issued when the delivery window is missed, subject to exclusion conditions (e.g., wrong customer address)." },
      { "term": "Driver Reassignment Window", "definition": "The 10-minute period within which a replacement driver must be assigned if the original driver cancels mid-delivery." },
      { "term": "Wrong Address", "definition": "A delivery address provided by the customer that is incorrect or unreachable, which voids the express refund eligibility." }
    ]
  },

  "business_rules": [
    {
      "id": "BR-1",
      "rule": "A customer may only select express delivery if their delivery address is within 10km of a warehouse.",
      "invariant": true,
      "invariant_justification": "This is a physical constraint — the 2-hour window is impossible to meet beyond this radius. Allowing ineligible orders would corrupt the service promise.",
      "testable_assertion": "Given a delivery address more than 10km from any warehouse, the express delivery option must not be selectable or submittable."
    },
    {
      "id": "BR-2",
      "rule": "Only drivers with a rating of 4.0 stars or above may be assigned to express deliveries.",
      "invariant": true,
      "invariant_justification": "Driver quality directly impacts the ability to meet the time-sensitive SLA. Assigning unqualified drivers corrupts the service tier's integrity.",
      "testable_assertion": "Given a driver with a rating below 4.0, that driver must never appear in the candidate pool for express order assignment."
    },
    {
      "id": "BR-3",
      "rule": "An express order must be picked up from the warehouse within 15 minutes of order placement.",
      "invariant": true,
      "invariant_justification": "Operations confirms the 2-hour delivery window cannot be met if pickup exceeds 15 minutes. This is a physical/temporal constraint, not a policy preference.",
      "testable_assertion": "Given an express order where pickup has not occurred within 15 minutes, the system must escalate (exact escalation action is undefined — see escalations)."
    },
    {
      "id": "BR-4",
      "rule": "The express fee is $12.99.",
      "invariant": false,
      "invariant_justification": "This is a pricing policy that can be changed by business decision without corrupting system state.",
      "testable_assertion": "Given a customer selecting express delivery, the express fee line item must equal $12.99."
    },
    {
      "id": "BR-5",
      "rule": "When a delivery window is missed, the express fee ($12.99) is refunded but the base delivery fee is not.",
      "invariant": false,
      "invariant_justification": "This is a refund policy, changeable by business decision. The scope of refund (express fee only vs full) is a business choice.",
      "testable_assertion": "Given a completed express delivery where actual delivery time exceeds 2 hours from order placement AND the customer address was valid, a refund of exactly the express fee amount must be initiated."
    },
    {
      "id": "BR-6",
      "rule": "No express fee refund is issued if the customer provided a wrong address.",
      "invariant": false,
      "invariant_justification": "This is a refund exclusion policy, not a data integrity constraint.",
      "testable_assertion": "Given a missed express delivery window where the delivery address was flagged as wrong/unreachable, no goodwill refund must be issued."
    },
    {
      "id": "BR-7",
      "rule": "Missed-window refunds are processed within 3 business days.",
      "invariant": false,
      "invariant_justification": "Processing time is an operational SLA, not a system invariant. Finance could change this window.",
      "testable_assertion": "Given a refund initiated for a missed express window, the refund must be completed within 3 business days of initiation."
    },
    {
      "id": "BR-8",
      "rule": "If a driver cancels mid-delivery on an express order, a replacement driver must be assigned within 10 minutes.",
      "invariant": false,
      "invariant_justification": "The 10-minute window is an operational target, not a physical constraint. It could be adjusted based on market conditions.",
      "testable_assertion": "Given an express order where the assigned driver cancels, a replacement express-qualified driver must be assigned within 10 minutes of cancellation."
    },
    {
      "id": "BR-9",
      "rule": "If no replacement driver is assigned within 10 minutes of a mid-delivery cancellation, an automatic refund of the express fee is triggered.",
      "invariant": false,
      "invariant_justification": "This is a customer-satisfaction policy, not a data integrity rule.",
      "testable_assertion": "Given an express order where the driver cancelled and 10 minutes elapsed with no replacement assigned, a refund of the express fee must be automatically initiated without manual intervention."
    },
    {
      "id": "BR-10",
      "rule": "Express customers must be able to track their assigned driver's location in real time.",
      "invariant": false,
      "invariant_justification": "Real-time tracking is a product feature, not a domain invariant. The system remains consistent without it.",
      "testable_assertion": "Given an active express delivery, the customer must see the driver's current location updated at least every 30 seconds (update frequency undefined — see escalations)."
    }
  ],

  "acceptance_criteria": [],

  "edge_cases": [
    { "scenario": "Customer is exactly 10km from a warehouse — boundary condition on eligibility.", "expected_behavior": "Must define whether 10km is inclusive or exclusive. Recommend inclusive (<=10km) for customer-friendly behavior, but this needs explicit decision." },
    { "scenario": "Driver rating drops below 4.0 stars mid-delivery on an express order.", "expected_behavior": "Undefined. The driver was qualified at assignment time. Business must decide: complete the delivery or reassign. Recommend: allow completion since reassignment would delay delivery." },
    { "scenario": "All express-qualified drivers are busy when an express order is placed.", "expected_behavior": "Undefined. Should the order be rejected at placement, queued, or downgraded to standard? This is a critical gap." },
    { "scenario": "Customer changes address after express order placement to a location >10km from warehouse.", "expected_behavior": "Undefined. Does this void express eligibility? Does it void refund eligibility? Needs a rule." },
    { "scenario": "Pickup happens at 14 minutes (within SLA) but traffic makes 2-hour delivery impossible.", "expected_behavior": "Pickup SLA was met but delivery window is still missed. Refund should still apply per BR-5 since the miss wasn't caused by customer." },
    { "scenario": "Who determines a 'wrong address' — the driver, the system, or a support agent?", "expected_behavior": "Undefined. The wrong-address exclusion (BR-6) is unenforceable without a clear adjudication process. Risk of driver abuse if drivers can unilaterally flag addresses to avoid refund penalties." },
    { "scenario": "Driver cancels at minute 115 of a 120-minute window — reassignment within 10 min is impossible before window expires.", "expected_behavior": "Two refund triggers overlap: missed window AND failed reassignment. Refund should be issued (only once), but the question is whether to even attempt reassignment or immediately refund." },
    { "scenario": "Multiple warehouse coverage — customer is within 10km of two warehouses.", "expected_behavior": "Undefined. Which warehouse fulfills? Nearest? Most inventory? This affects pickup SLA feasibility." },
    { "scenario": "Express order placed at 11:50 PM — does the 2-hour window span midnight or are there operating hours?", "expected_behavior": "Undefined. No mention of operating hours or cut-off times for express availability." }
  ],

  "business_impact": {
    "financial": "Express fee ($12.99) creates a new revenue stream on eligible orders. Refund exposure is significant if operational SLAs (15-min pickup, 2-hour delivery) are frequently missed — each miss is a $12.99 direct cost plus potential customer churn. The automatic refund on failed reassignment adds uncontrolled refund liability if driver cancellation rates are high.",
    "operational": "Express creates a two-tier driver pool (4+ stars vs below), which reduces effective driver supply for express orders. The 15-minute pickup SLA requires warehouse operations to prioritize express orders, potentially delaying standard orders. The 10-minute reassignment window demands a real-time driver matching system with reserve capacity.",
    "user": "Positive: faster delivery option with real-time tracking improves customer experience. Negative: the Legal/PM contradiction on 'guarantee' vs 'best effort' creates a trust risk — marketing says 'guaranteed' but terms say 'best effort.' Customers who see 'guaranteed' and then receive a refund instead of on-time delivery will feel deceived.",
    "risk": "HIGH — The guarantee vs best-effort contradiction is a brand and legal liability. If marketed as a guarantee but disclaimed as best-effort, this invites regulatory scrutiny and class-action risk in consumer-protection-heavy jurisdictions. The wrong-address exclusion without a clear adjudication process risks customer disputes and inconsistent enforcement.",
    "stakeholders_affected": [
      { "group": "Customers", "impact": "Gain a premium delivery option but face confusion between 'guaranteed' marketing and 'best effort' legal terms. Refund exclusions (wrong address) may cause disputes." },
      { "group": "Drivers", "impact": "Only 4+ star drivers qualify, creating a tiered workforce. Express orders add time pressure. Mid-delivery cancellation triggers a 10-minute reassignment clock that affects replacement drivers." },
      { "group": "Warehouse Operations", "impact": "Must prioritize express order picking to meet the 15-minute pickup SLA. May need dedicated express lanes or staff." },
      { "group": "Finance", "impact": "New revenue stream offset by refund liability. Must build refund processing within 3 business days. Need to track express P&L separately." },
      { "group": "Customer Support", "impact": "Will handle disputes around wrong-address refund denials, missed windows, and the guarantee vs best-effort gap. Need clear escalation playbooks." },
      { "group": "Legal/Compliance", "impact": "Must resolve the guarantee language before launch. Marketing materials, terms of service, and in-app copy must be aligned." }
    ]
  },

  "confidence": {
    "level": 40,
    "reasoning": "Confidence is low due to a fundamental contradiction between PM ('guaranteed') and Legal ('best effort, not a guarantee') that affects the core product promise, refund policy enforceability, and marketing language. Additionally, several critical operational flows are undefined (escalation on missed pickup SLA, no-driver-available scenario, wrong-address adjudication). Until the guarantee vs best-effort conflict is resolved, acceptance criteria cannot be reliably defined.",
    "high_confidence_areas": [
      "Express fee amount ($12.99) is agreed upon",
      "10km eligibility radius is clear and uncontested",
      "4-star driver minimum is clear and uncontested",
      "Refund scope (express fee only, not base fee) is agreed upon",
      "Wrong-address exclusion intent is clear, even if process is undefined"
    ],
    "low_confidence_areas": [
      "Whether the 2-hour window is a guarantee or best-effort SLA — this is a direct contradiction",
      "What happens when pickup SLA is breached (escalation path undefined)",
      "What happens when no express-qualified drivers are available",
      "How 'wrong address' is determined and by whom",
      "Whether the automatic refund on failed reassignment conflicts with the 3-business-day processing window",
      "Operating hours and geographic rollout scope",
      "Real-time tracking update frequency and failure tolerance"
    ],
    "assumptions": [
      "The 2-hour window starts at order placement time, not at pickup time",
      "The 10km distance is calculated as straight-line (geodesic), not driving distance — this needs clarification",
      "Driver rating is their current aggregate rating, not a per-delivery score",
      "A single warehouse serves each express order (no multi-warehouse fulfillment)",
      "The 3-business-day refund processing applies to all express refunds including automatic ones from failed reassignment"
    ]
  },

  "escalations": [
    {
      "type": "contradiction",
      "description": "PM states the 2-hour delivery is 'guaranteed.' Legal explicitly states it cannot be guaranteed in writing and must be framed as 'best effort.' This is a fundamental contradiction that affects the entire product positioning, marketing copy, in-app language, refund policy framing (goodwill vs obligation), and potential legal exposure.",
      "affected_stakeholders": ["Customers", "Legal/Compliance", "Marketing", "Customer Support"],
      "options": [
        "A: Frame as 'guarantee' with contractual backing — accept legal risk, deliver on-time or refund as obligation",
        "B: Frame as 'best effort target' with goodwill refund — reduces legal risk but weakens product appeal",
        "C: Frame as 'Express Promise' — marketing-friendly language that avoids the word 'guarantee' while implying strong commitment, with refund as standard policy (not legal obligation)"
      ],
      "recommendation": "Option C — 'Express Promise' balances marketing appeal with legal safety. But this MUST be resolved before any acceptance criteria can be finalized, as it determines whether refunds are contractual obligations or discretionary goodwill."
    },
    {
      "type": "ambiguity",
      "description": "No defined escalation path when the 15-minute pickup SLA is breached. Should the order be auto-cancelled? Reassigned to another warehouse? Should the customer be notified? Should a refund be pre-emptively issued? Operations says the 2-hour window can't be met, but doesn't say what happens next.",
      "affected_stakeholders": ["Customers", "Warehouse Operations", "Drivers"],
      "options": [
        "A: Auto-reassign to another warehouse if available",
        "B: Notify customer of delay and offer cancellation",
        "C: Continue delivery attempt but pre-flag as likely-missed-window for proactive refund"
      ],
      "recommendation": "Option C with customer notification — don't cancel proactively (customer may still want the delivery), but set expectations and prepare the refund pipeline."
    },
    {
      "type": "ambiguity",
      "description": "No rule for what happens when no express-qualified drivers (4+ stars) are available at order placement time. This is a critical flow for launch — high demand or low driver supply could make this common.",
      "affected_stakeholders": ["Customers", "Operations", "Finance"],
      "options": [
        "A: Reject the express option at checkout (show as unavailable)",
        "B: Accept the order and queue it, hoping a driver becomes available within the 15-minute pickup window",
        "C: Temporarily lower the driver rating threshold"
      ],
      "recommendation": "Option A — do not accept an express order you cannot fulfill. Show express as unavailable when no qualified drivers are in range. Option C violates BR-2 and should be rejected."
    },
    {
      "type": "ambiguity",
      "description": "The 'wrong address' refund exclusion (BR-6) lacks an adjudication process. Who determines a wrong address? What evidence is required? Without this, the rule is unenforceable and open to abuse (drivers flagging addresses to avoid accountability, or customers disputing the determination).",
      "affected_stakeholders": ["Customers", "Drivers", "Customer Support"],
      "options": [
        "A: Driver flags wrong address, customer support reviews and decides",
        "B: System validates address at order time (geocoding), wrong-address exclusion only applies if customer overrode a validation warning",
        "C: Driver provides photo evidence, automated review with support escalation"
      ],
      "recommendation": "Option B — validate upfront and only exclude refunds when the customer bypassed a system warning. This is the most defensible and least dispute-prone approach."
    },
    {
      "type": "missing_context",
      "description": "No operating hours defined for express delivery. Can a customer place an express order at 2 AM? Are warehouses staffed 24/7? The 2-hour window becomes meaningless without operating context.",
      "affected_stakeholders": ["Customers", "Warehouse Operations"],
      "options": [
        "A: Express available 24/7 (requires 24/7 warehouse staffing and driver availability)",
        "B: Express available during defined hours (e.g., 8 AM – 10 PM)",
        "C: Express availability based on real-time warehouse and driver capacity"
      ],
      "recommendation": "Option C for launch — dynamically show express as available only when warehouse is operational and drivers are in range. Avoids over-promising."
    },
    {
      "type": "missing_context",
      "description": "The 10km eligibility radius is undefined in measurement method. Straight-line (geodesic) distance vs actual driving distance can differ by 30-50% in urban areas. A customer 10km straight-line could be 15km by road, making the 2-hour window infeasible.",
      "affected_stakeholders": ["Customers", "Operations"],
      "options": [
        "A: Use straight-line distance (simpler but less accurate)",
        "B: Use driving distance (more accurate but computationally heavier)",
        "C: Use straight-line with a conservative radius (e.g., 8km straight-line ≈ 10km driving)"
      ],
      "recommendation": "Option B — driving distance. The eligibility check happens once at order time, so computational cost is trivial. Using straight-line will produce edge cases where express is offered but undeliverable."
    }
  ],

  "rejection_reasons": []
}
